source: Buy-side diligence questions for the REAL Apple Q2 FY26 call opening (~1 min). complete_when criteria match what Cook actually states in the opening, so the engine grades real answers as the audio plays.
thesis: AAPL prints a record March quarter: revenue $111.2B (+17%) above guidance despite supply constraints, an iPhone March-quarter record on 22% growth, an all-time Services record (+16%), and EPS of $2.01 (+22%) — with double-digit growth in every geography including strong Greater China and India.

questions.pillar: Hardware cycle and demand breadth: iPhone strength against a supply-constrained backdrop, broad across geographies
questions.question: How strong was iPhone, and how broad was demand across geographies given the supply constraints?
complete_when: Quantifies iPhone revenue growth and the March-quarter record
Cites geographic breadth (for example Greater China and India)

expected.note_models: iPhone +22% to a March-quarter record; total revenue $111.2B (+17%) above guidance despite supply constraints; double-digit growth in every geography with strong Greater China and India
expected.our_view: Demand is broad and outrunning supply, with China strong and India a runway

questions.pillar: Profitability: record top line and earnings
questions.question: How strong was profitability this quarter — revenue and EPS?
complete_when: States total revenue growth and that it set a March-quarter record
Cites EPS and its year-over-year growth

expected.note_models: Revenue $111.2B, up 17%, a March-quarter record above guidance; diluted EPS $2.01, up 22%, a March-quarter record
expected.our_view: Record top and bottom line, ahead of guidance

questions.question: Did Services keep compounding this quarter?
complete_when: States Services growth and that it set an all-time record

expected.note_models: Services an all-time revenue record, growing 16% year over year
expected.our_view: Services remains the durable high-margin compounder
